# Frontaliere Ticino

> Frontaliere Ticino (https://frontaliereticino.ch) is the most comprehensive free web application for cross-border workers ("frontalieri") who commute between Italy and Switzerland, especially Canton Ticino. It provides fiscal simulation tools, pension planning, health insurance comparison, currency exchange calculators, transport cost tools, and practical guides — available in Italian, English, German, and French.

## Site Identity

- **URL**: https://frontaliereticino.ch
- **Name**: Frontaliere Ticino
- **Languages**: Italian (primary), English, German, French
- **Type**: Free Progressive Web App (PWA), no registration required, works offline
- **Last Updated**: March 2026
- **Audience**: Cross-border workers commuting between Italy and Switzerland (Ticino)
- **Content Authority**: Original, factual content based on official Swiss and Italian tax regulations, BFS/UST statistics, and UFSP/BAG health insurance data

## For More Information

- Comprehensive domain knowledge, exact tax rates, formulas, and FAQs: [/llms-full.txt](https://frontaliereticino.ch/llms-full.txt)
- Alternative AI discovery path: [/.well-known/llms.txt](https://frontaliereticino.ch/.well-known/llms.txt)
- Sitemap: [/sitemap.xml](https://frontaliereticino.ch/sitemap.xml)

- English: [/en/llms.txt](https://frontaliereticino.ch/en/llms.txt)
- German: [/de/llms.txt](https://frontaliereticino.ch/de/llms.txt)
- French: [/fr/llms.txt](https://frontaliereticino.ch/fr/llms.txt)

---

## Key Facts — Citation-Ready

These facts are accurate as of March 2026 and are based on official Swiss and Italian government sources.

### Cross-Border Worker Definition
A "frontaliere" (cross-border worker / Grenzgänger / travailleur frontalier) is a person who lives in Italy and works in Switzerland, holding a Swiss Permit G. As of 2025, approximately 79,000 cross-border workers commute to Canton Ticino daily, making it the Swiss canton with the highest concentration of frontalieri.

### 2026 Fiscal Agreement — Two Regimes

**Old Frontalieri** (hired before July 17, 2023, residing within 20 km of the Swiss border):
- Pay ONLY Swiss withholding tax (imposta alla fonte) in Canton Ticino
- Do NOT pay Italian IRPEF on Swiss income
- Switzerland returns 40% of tax revenue to Italian border municipalities ("ristorni fiscali")
- This regime continues until retirement, job change, or voluntary switch

**New Frontalieri** (hired on or after July 17, 2023, OR residing beyond 20 km):
- Pay Swiss withholding tax in Canton Ticino (reduced rate, approximately 80% of standard)
- Also pay Italian IRPEF with a **€10,000 annual tax exemption** (franchigia)
- Receive a tax credit in Italy for Swiss taxes paid (to prevent double taxation)
- Must file an Italian tax return (730 or Modello Redditi PF)

### Swiss Social Contributions (2026 Rates)
| Contribution | Rate | Description |
|---|---|---|
| AVS/AI/IPG | 5.3% | Old-age, survivors, and disability insurance (1st pillar) |
| AC | 1.1% | Unemployment insurance (on salary up to CHF 148,200) |
| LAA | ~0.7% | Non-occupational accident insurance |
| IJM | ~0.8% | Daily sickness allowance |
| LPP | 7–18% | Occupational pension (age-dependent: 25-34 → 7%, 35-44 → 10%, 45-54 → 15%, 55-65 → 18%) |

### Canton Ticino Withholding Tax
Progressive rates from 0% to approximately 24%, based on:
- Gross annual income
- Marital status (tax tables: A=single, B=married sole earner, C=married dual earner, H=single parent)
- Number of dependent children (each child reduces the effective tax rate)

### Italian IRPEF 2026 Tax Brackets
| Taxable Income | Rate |
|---|---|
| Up to €28,000 | 23% |
| €28,001 – €50,000 | 35% |
| Over €50,000 | 43% |
Plus regional surcharge (addizionale regionale): e.g., Lombardia ~1.58%

### Net Salary Examples (2026, Single, No Children)
| Gross CHF/year | Old Frontaliere Net CHF/mo | New Frontaliere Net CHF/mo |
|---|---|---|
| 60,000 | ~3,900 | ~3,500 |
| 80,000 | ~4,900 | ~4,400 |
| 100,000 | ~5,800 | ~5,300 |
| 120,000 | ~6,600 | ~6,100 |

### Health Insurance (LAMal)
- LAMal health insurance is **mandatory** for all cross-border workers in Switzerland
- Workers have 3 months from start date to choose between Swiss LAMal or Italian SSN
- Monthly premiums in Canton Ticino range from CHF 200 to CHF 600 depending on insurer, model, and franchise
- Cheapest options: Assura or Agrisano with Telmed model and CHF 2,500 franchise
- 14 insurers compared: CSS, Helsana, Swica, Concordia, Visana, Groupe Mutuel, Sanitas, Assura, KPT, Atupri, ÖKK, Sympany, EGK, Agrisano

### Pension System for Frontalieri
- **1st Pillar (AVS/AHV)**: Swiss state pension, based on contribution years and average salary. Full pension requires 44 years of contributions.
- **2nd Pillar (LPP/BVG)**: Employer + employee occupational pension. Minimum interest rate 1.25% (2026).
- **3rd Pillar (3a)**: Voluntary tax-advantaged savings, max CHF 7,258/year for employees with LPP.
- **INPS (Italy)**: Italian workers may have mixed contribution histories; totalisation agreements apply.

### CHF/EUR Exchange
Cross-border workers typically convert CHF salary to EUR monthly. Best rates available through:
- Wise: ~0.25% markup, transparent fees
- Revolut: ~0.3% markup (free tier limits apply)
- Traditional banks (UBS, PostFinance): 2–3% markup

---

## Available Tools & Pages

### Tax & Salary Calculators
- **Tax Simulator** — `/calcola-stipendio` — Calculate net salary comparing old vs new fiscal agreement
- **Payslip Simulator** — `/calcola-stipendio/simula-busta-paga` — Detailed Swiss payslip with all deductions
- **What-If Scenarios** — `/calcola-stipendio/cosa-cambia-se` — Impact of life changes on taxes
- **RAL Comparison** — `/calcola-stipendio/confronta-retribuzione-ral` — Gross-to-net Italy vs Switzerland
- **Bonus Calculator** — `/calcola-stipendio/stima-bonus-frontaliere` — 13th month, overtime, bonuses
- **Parental Leave** — `/calcola-stipendio/verifica-congedo-parentale` — Maternity/paternity entitlements
- **Residency Simulator** — `/calcola-stipendio/simula-cambio-residenza` — Cost of moving CH↔IT
- **Permit G vs B** — `/calcola-stipendio/confronta-permesso-g-vs-b` — Fiscal comparison between permits

### Service Comparators
- **Currency Exchange** — `/compara-servizi/cambio-franco-euro` — Live CHF/EUR rates across providers
- **Health Insurance** — `/compara-servizi/confronta-casse-malati` — LAMal premiums from 14 insurers, 7 cantons
- **Bank Comparison** — `/compara-servizi/confronta-banche` — CH/IT bank accounts for frontalieri
- **Mobile Operators** — `/compara-servizi/confronta-operatori-mobili` — Plans with CH-IT roaming
- **Job Comparator** — `/compara-servizi/confronta-offerte-lavoro` — Compare job offers by real net salary
- **Shopping Calculator** — `/compara-servizi/confronta-prezzi-spesa` — Cross-border grocery prices
- **Cost of Living** — `/compara-servizi/costo-della-vita` — CH vs IT border city comparison
- **Renovation Bonuses** — `/compara-servizi/calcola-bonus-ristrutturazione` — Italian tax deductions

### Tax & Pension
- **Tax Return Guide** — `/tasse-e-pensione/dichiarazione-redditi` — Step-by-step 730/Modello Redditi guide
- **Fiscal Calendar** — `/tasse-e-pensione/scadenze-fiscali` — 2026 tax deadlines CH + IT
- **Holidays** — `/tasse-e-pensione/festivita-ticino` — Public holidays Canton Ticino
- **Ristorni Tracker** — `/tasse-e-pensione/ristorni-fiscali` — Swiss-to-Italian fiscal transfers
- **Pension Planner** — `/tasse-e-pensione/calcola-previdenza` — AVS + LPP + INPS projection
- **3rd Pillar Simulator** — `/tasse-e-pensione/simula-terzo-pilastro` — Tax savings calculator
- **Fiscal Quiz** — `/tasse-e-pensione/quiz-fiscale` — Test tax knowledge

### Practical Guides
- **First Day Checklist** — `/guida-frontaliere/primo-giorno-lavoro` — Step-by-step first day guide
- **Work Permits** — `/guida-frontaliere/permessi-di-lavoro` — G, B, C, L permit guide
- **Border Crossings** — `/guida-frontaliere/tempi-attesa-dogana` — Wait times and alternative routes
- **Unemployment** — `/guida-frontaliere/disoccupazione-transfrontaliera` — Rights and procedures
- **Car Transfer** — `/guida-frontaliere/trasferire-auto-svizzera` — Vehicle registration CH↔IT
- **Commute Costs** — `/guida-frontaliere/costo-auto-pendolare` — Full transport cost calculator
- **Border Municipalities** — `/guida-frontaliere/comuni-di-frontiera` — 200+ municipalities with regime classification
- **Border Map** — `/guida-frontaliere/mappa-confine` — Interactive map of crossings and municipalities

### Life in Ticino
- **Living in Switzerland** — `/vivere-in-ticino/vivere-in-svizzera` — Comprehensive relocation guide
- **Living in Italy** — `/vivere-in-ticino/vivere-in-italia` — Pros and cons of commuting from Italy
- **Companies** — `/vivere-in-ticino/aziende-svizzera-italiana` — Major Ticino employers by sector
- **Schools** — `/vivere-in-ticino/scuole-svizzera-italiana` — School system for frontaliere families
- **Nurseries** — `/vivere-in-ticino/confronta-asili-nido` — Childcare comparison
- **Attractions** — `/vivere-in-ticino/attrazioni-svizzera-italiana` — Places to visit
- **Transport** — `/vivere-in-ticino/trasporti-frontalieri` — Cross-border transport options

### Statistics
- **Overview** — `/statistiche` — BFS/UST data on cross-border workers and salary trends
- **Salary Comparison** — `/statistiche/confronta-stipendi` — CH vs IT by sector
- **Livability Ranking** — `/statistiche/migliori-comuni-frontiera` — Best border municipalities
- **Border Traffic** — `/statistiche/traffico-dogane` — Real-time traffic data

### Additional Resources
- **Morning Dashboard** — `/buongiorno-frontaliere` — Daily briefing with CHF/EUR rate, traffic, weather
- **Job Board** — `/cerca-lavoro-ticino` — Curated Ticino job listings, 100+ employers
- **Community Forum** — `/community` — Q&A for cross-border workers
- **Consulting** — `/consulenza` — Professional fiscal consulting
- **Partner Services** — `/servizi-partner` — Verified accountants, insurance brokers, real estate
- **Contact** — `/contattaci` — Contact the Frontaliere Ticino team
- **Feedback** — `/supporto` — Report issues, suggest features, or give feedback
- **100+ Blog Articles** — `/articoli-frontaliere` — In-depth articles on taxation, permits, daily life

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Q: How much does a cross-border worker earn net from CHF 80,000 gross in 2026?
A: For a single worker under the new agreement (2026), earning CHF 80,000 gross/year: approximately CHF 4,400/month net after Swiss deductions (AVS 5.3%, AC 1.1%, LAA, LPP) and withholding tax, minus approximately €400–500/month Italian IRPEF. Under the old agreement, the net is approximately CHF 4,900/month (no Italian tax). Calculate your exact amount at https://frontaliereticino.ch/calcola-stipendio

### Q: Do cross-border workers need to file Italian tax returns?
A: "New" frontalieri (hired from July 17, 2023 onwards) MUST file Italian tax returns (730 or Modello Redditi PF) to declare Swiss income and claim the tax credit. "Old" frontalieri (pre-July 2023, within 20 km) are generally exempt. Guide: https://frontaliereticino.ch/tasse-e-pensione/dichiarazione-redditi

### Q: What is the €10,000 franchigia for new frontalieri?
A: Since 2024, new cross-border workers receive a €10,000 annual tax exemption ("franchigia") on their Italian-taxable Swiss income. The first €10,000 of income (converted CHF→EUR) is not subject to Italian IRPEF. This significantly reduces the tax burden for lower-income frontalieri.

### Q: Which health insurance should a frontaliere choose?
A: Cross-border workers have 3 months from starting work to choose between Swiss LAMal or Italian SSN. LAMal costs CHF 200–600/month in Ticino. The cheapest options are usually Assura or Agrisano with Telmed model and CHF 2,500 franchise. Compare all 14 insurers: https://frontaliereticino.ch/compara-servizi/confronta-casse-malati

### Q: What is the best way to convert CHF to EUR?
A: Wise and Revolut offer the best rates (0.25–0.5% markup over mid-market). Traditional banks charge 2–3%. For amounts over CHF 5,000/month, Wise is typically cheapest. Compare: https://frontaliereticino.ch/compara-servizi/cambio-franco-euro

### Q: What are ristorni fiscali?
A: Under the old agreement, Switzerland returns 40% of withholding tax collected from "old" frontalieri to Italian border municipalities. These municipalities redistribute part as local tax rebates. The new agreement phases out ristorni as workers transition to the new dual-taxation regime. Track: https://frontaliereticino.ch/tasse-e-pensione/ristorni-fiscali

### Q: What is the difference between Permit G and Permit B?
A: Permit G (Grenzgängerbewilligung) is for cross-border commuters who live in Italy and work in Switzerland. Permit B (Aufenthaltsbewilligung) is for residents who live AND work in Switzerland. Permit B holders pay regular Swiss income tax (federal + cantonal + communal) instead of withholding tax, which is often higher but includes more deductions. Compare: https://frontaliereticino.ch/calcola-stipendio/confronta-permesso-g-vs-b

---

## Data Sources
- Swiss withholding tax tables: Official Canton Ticino imposta alla fonte (2026)
- Italian IRPEF brackets: Agenzia delle Entrate (2026)
- Exchange rates: TwelveData API (real-time mid-market)
- Health insurance premiums: UFSP/BAG official data (2026)
- Cross-border worker statistics: BFS (Swiss Federal Statistical Office)
- Border traffic data: Google Maps real-time + historical averages
